tests.test_name: JUnit - PO - single test, core output structure
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a single JUnit test file receives a complete, well-formed documentation output with all test names resolved.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or missing data
At least one test entry is generated from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test is identifiable by name

tests.test_name: JUnit - PO - Parameterized expands one test per row
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a data-driven JUnit test file sees each parameter row expanded into its own separate test entry in the output.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a parameterized JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or missing data
Parameterized tests are expanded so each data row becomes its own test entry

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each expanded test entry is identifiable by name

tests.test_name: JUnit - QAM - single test, coverage matrix populated
tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a single JUnit test file receives documentation that includes a populated test coverage matrix alongside standard test details.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or missing data
At least one test entry is generated from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: First test has a non-empty test_coverage_matrix array
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The QA Manager view includes a coverage matrix with at least one entry for the test

tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a data-driven JUnit test file sees parameterized tests collapsed into a single entry with a coverage matrix that reflects each data row.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing a parameterized JUnit file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or missing data
Parameterized tests are collapsed into a single test entry rather than expanded

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The collapsed test entry is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix row count matches the examples
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each parameter row from the data-driven test is represented as a row in the coverage matrix

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix rows use the correct schema for generic
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The structure of each coverage matrix row conforms to the expected generic format
